description: Reconstructed JSON for the architecture stub-fill batch. The original subagent stalled after drafting all 7 notes but before writing this file; orchestrator derived the 5 missing paper-keys by auditing arch-note citations against papers.json and added existing-paper updates for keys whose excerpts_file the agent created on disk.

new_papers.title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning
new_papers.authors: Alayrac, Donahue, Luc, Miech, Barr, Hasson, Lenc, Mensch, Millican, Reynolds, Ring, Rutherford, Cabi, Han, Gong, Samangooei, Monteiro, Menick, Borgeaud, Brock, Nematzadeh, Sharifzadeh, Binkowski, Barreira, Vinyals, Zisserman, Simonyan
new_papers.summary: Flamingo: bridge a frozen vision encoder to a frozen LM via Perceiver Resampler + interleaved gated cross-attention layers; trained on interleaved image-text web data. Establishes the cross-attention-bridge architectural family for multimodal LLMs.

new_papers.title: Layer Normalization
new_papers.summary: LayerNorm: per-token normalization across the hidden dimension with learned gain and bias. The original normalization the canonical Transformer used; later largely superseded in LLMs by RMSNorm but the conceptual baseline.

new_papers.title: Better & Faster Large Language Models via Multi-token Prediction
new_papers.summary: Trains an LM with N parallel output heads predicting the next 1..N tokens jointly. Improves sample efficiency at scale and provides a built-in self-speculative-decoding hook. Distinct from speculative decoding (which uses a separate draft model).

new_papers.title: SentencePiece: A simple and language independent subword tokenizer and detokenizer for Neural Text Processing
new_papers.summary: Whitespace-agnostic, byte-level-capable subword tokenizer + detokenizer. Treats raw text as a Unicode byte stream and learns BPE or unigram-LM subwords on it. Becomes the standard tokenizer scaffolding for multilingual LMs.

new_papers.title: Using the Output Embedding to Improve Language Models
new_papers.summary: Tied weight scheme: share the input token embedding matrix with the output unembedding. Cuts ~30% of parameters in small LMs and improves PPL by serving as an implicit regularizer. The default in many decoder-only LMs through ~2023; modern frontier LMs sometimes untie.
